real_world_scenario.name: Enterprise Copilot with Mixed Interactive and Long-Context Requests
real_world_scenario.description: This workload models a production copilot where short conversational turns, follow-up clarifications, and rewrite requests arrive continuously while a smaller number of document-grounded long-context jobs are also admitted. Examples include support copilots, internal knowledge assistants, and document QA systems that mix chat turns with report summarization, meeting QA, and multi-hop retrieval.
why_real: Short interactive turns are represented by open chat corpora such as UltraChat.
Long-context jobs are represented by LongBench tasks such as report summarization and question answering over long contexts.
Poisson arrivals approximate independent user arrivals under bursty but memoryless traffic.
Beneficiary-rich mixed ordering captures realistic periods where long jobs are already in flight when short turns arrive.

models.label: Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2
models.reason: Instruction-tuned sibling of the same family.

models.label: Gemma-7B-IT
models.reason: Non-Mistral architecture and strongest historical beneficiary-rich case.

models.label: Baichuan2-7B-Chat
models.reason: Non-Mistral, non-Gemma chat model kept for architecture diversity.

datasets.label: UltraChat200k
datasets.reason: Short and medium-length conversational turns resembling online assistant traffic.

datasets.label: LongBench
datasets.reason: Long prompts such as report summarization and long-context QA.

densities.name: low
densities.reason: Responsiveness-focused operating band without persistent saturation.

densities.name: mid
densities.reason: Main operating band where short interactive turns and long jobs overlap frequently.

densities.name: high
densities.reason: Higher-load band that stresses responsiveness under mixed long and short arrivals.
